source: Deep Matrix Factorization Models for Recommender Systems

concepts.finding: Deep MF: extends PMF with deep neural network to learn nonlinear user-item interactions. Unlike linear MF, deep architecture captures complex interaction patterns. Three architectures compared: (1) deep user network + deep item network, (2) deep user + linear item, (3) deep on combined features. Deep+Deep performs best but requires most data.
concepts.implication: Nonlinear keyword-intent interactions captured by deep MF. Linear embedding (MF) provides good baseline; deep network captures non-obvious patterns (e.g., keyword combinations that signal specific intents). Deep network needs sufficient training data.
concepts.open_question: Are keyword-intent interactions predominantly linear (captured by dot product) or nonlinear (requiring deep networks)?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: A deep MF model for keyword-intent scoring captures nonlinear patterns that improve recall@K by 5%+ over linear MF, especially for multi-intent keywords.
